transplant_record.rationale: rome-patches-canonical.md v0.1 §1.3 'shared codebase / transitively applied' claim was empirically falsified at Cell 2 first execution. Patches P-1, P-2, P-4 (dual site), P-5, P-6 are NOT in upstream kmeng01/rome at SHA 0874014cd9837e4365f3e6f3c71400ef11509e04 — they are project-side fork-work applied to MEMIT-side /workspace/memit_dry_run/memit at S2.3/S2.5a. Diff content matches v0.1 §1 declared semantics exactly; transplant is byte-for-byte the MEMIT-side patched form. Routes to v0.2 patches doc applicability recategorization.

v0_1_undocumented_findings.summary: MEMIT-side rome/layer_stats.py adds force_recompute=False parameter to layer_stats() signature and gates cache=filename via (filename if not force_recompute else None). Functionally inert under v0.1 runbook usage (force_recompute defaults to False, preserving canonical caching). Document at v0.2 for completeness; informational only.

P-4 patch produces redundant nested expression: getattr(model.config, 'hidden_size', None) or (getattr(model.config, 'hidden_size', None) or model.config.n_embd). Functionally equivalent to single getattr(...) or model.config.n_embd. Authoring redundancy; cleanup candidate at v0.2 patch reauthoring.

Config-layer patch class missed at v0.1: ROME's util/globals.py reads globals.yml at cwd-relative path on import; STATS_DIR upstream is 'data/stats' (relative). MEMIT-side was customized to absolute /workspace/covariance_caches at pre-S2.6 fork-work; ROME-side never was. S2.18 Cell 8 R1.3 smoke would have triggered local Wikipedia compute fallback (~30 min wall-time per layer; the exact failure class R1.3 was designed to detect) without this patch. v0.2 must introduce CONFIG_LAYER patch class and document this customization.

rome-patches-canonical.md v0.1 §1.3 P-6 entry claims 'ROME does call get_cov during edit dispatch'. Empirically false: ROME's compute_u.py defines get_inv_cov instead (returns torch.inverse of mom2 matrix; signature: model, tok, layer_name, mom2_dataset, mom2_n_samples, mom2_dtype). v0.2 §1.3 P-7 inapplicability rationale must be re-authored: ROME-equivalent of MEMIT's R1.3 dispatch test is get_inv_cov, not get_cov.

Cell 4 v0.2 OQ resolved: ROME's compute_u.py L109 uses 'if hparams.mom2_adjustment:' as a truthy gate; integer values like 15000 are functionally equivalent to True. Schema-correct value at v0.2 is 'mom2_adjustment: True'. Current S2.18 value 15000 is harmless dead weight. v0.2 hparam dict authoring should change to bool.

Cell 4 hparam 'stats_dir' field is empirically NEVER consumed by ROME's edit dispatch (layer_stats() positional STATS_DIR comes from util.globals at module-import time). Genuine dead weight. v0.2 hparam dict can drop this field. REVISED at P-7-ROME application: 'mom2_batch_tokens' is NOT dead weight — it was load-bearing but unused due to source defect. Now consumed via P-7-ROME at compute_u call site.

v0.1 §1.3 P-7 INAPPLICABLE declaration is empirically wrong. ROME has analogous defect class to MEMIT's P-7, just on get_inv_cov instead of get_cov. P-7-ROME applied at Cell 8 first execution: 3 surgical edits thread mom2_batch_tokens through compute_u → get_inv_cov → layer_stats. Empirical anchor: pre-P-7-ROME cache lookup constructed `_mom2_100000.npz` (404 + OOM); post-P-7-ROME expected to construct `_mom2_t100_100000.npz` (cache HIT). v0.2 patches doc must add P-7-ROME entry; recategorize P-7 from INAPPLICABLE to APPLICABLE-WITH-MODIFICATION (different file: rome/compute_u.py rather than memit/memit_main.py).

P-8-ROME: precision coercion at compute_u matmul. ROME's get_inv_cov returns fp32 (.float() cast); fp16 model produces fp16 u vector; matmul fails with dtype mismatch RuntimeError. Fix: `.to(u.dtype)` cast before matmul. ROME-only patch (MEMIT bypasses this path entirely via memit_main.compute_u_v / compute_z). v0.2 patches doc: NEW source-layer patch class P-8-ROME; latent defect in upstream kmeng01/rome on any fp16 base model; was never observed because ROME paper used GPT-2-XL/GPT-J fp32.

P-9-ROME: precision coercion at outer-product. Two sites: execute_rome L117 + apply_rome_to_model L43. left_vector/delta_u from compute_u is fp16 (post-P-8-ROME); right_vector/delta_v from compute_v is fp32 (Adam optimization buffer); matmul fp16 @ fp32 fails. Fix: cast both operands to weight.dtype before matmul. Sibling of P-8-ROME (same class, different file); ROME-only (MEMIT bypasses rome_main.py entirely).

globals_yml.rationale: v0.1 patches doc inventory missed config-layer patches entirely. ROME upstream globals.yml has cwd-relative STATS_DIR='data/stats'; post-customization STATS_DIR='/workspace/covariance_caches' to match the NV-resident cache directory. Parallel to MEMIT-side pre-S2.6 fork-work customization but applied to a structurally-different globals.yml schema (4 paths vs 5; no KV_DIR). v0.2 patches doc finding: introduce CONFIG_LAYER patch class; document this customization.

P-7-ROME.rationale: Empirical correction to v0.1 §1.3 P-7 entry. v0.1 declared P-7 INAPPLICABLE to ROME because 'ROME's dispatch path through rome_main.py does not call get_cov with the same batched-projection requirement.' Empirical finding at Cell 8 R1.3-equivalent first execution (S2.18 SHA 0874014c): ROME has its own analogous defect class via get_inv_cov in rome/compute_u.py — get_inv_cov calls layer_stats(...) WITHOUT threading batch_tokens through, so P-6's _t100_ filename segment is not produced (P-6 gate is `if batch_tokens < npos`; default batch_tokens = npos * 3 = 393216, guard fails). Result: cache lookup constructs `_mom2_100000.npz` instead of `_mom2_t100_100000.npz`, gets HTTP 404 from upstream rome.baulab.info, falls back to local compute, OOMs on RTX 4090 with 16 GB model resident. P-7-ROME is the ROME analog of P-7: thread batch_tokens through get_inv_cov → layer_stats. Three surgical edits: (1) get_inv_cov signature gains mom2_batch_tokens parameter, (2) layer_stats call inside get_inv_cov passes batch_tokens=mom2_batch_tokens, (3) compute_u call site passes hparams.mom2_batch_tokens via getattr.

P-8-ROME.rationale: Empirical finding at Cell 9 first execution. ROME's compute_u line 120 does `u = get_inv_cov(...) @ u.unsqueeze(1)`. get_inv_cov returns torch.inverse(...).float() (fp32 by L52-53 cast); u is cur_repr extracted from a fp16 model → c10::Half. The `@` operator requires matching dtypes; RuntimeError fires. P-8-ROME adds `.to(u.dtype)` cast on the inv_cov return value at the matmul site. MEMIT-side rome/compute_u.py has the SAME defect (no precision coercion); MEMIT doesn't hit the path because apply_memit_to_model uses memit/memit_main's compute_u_v with compute_z (not rome/compute_u.py at all). So the latent defect in MEMIT-side rome/compute_u.py never surfaced empirically. P-8-ROME is therefore ROME-specific and has NO MEMIT analog. v0.2 patches doc must add P-8-ROME entry as a NEW source-layer patch, marked ROME-only (no MEMIT applicability).

P-9-ROME.rationale: Empirical finding at Cell 9 retry. After P-8-ROME fixes the compute_u matmul site, ROME proceeds through compute_v's full 20-iteration optimization (loss 18.173→4.928; P(guitar) avg-prob 1.3e-08→1.06e-02 over the optimization trace at the rewrite layer). Then RuntimeError fires at rome_main.py L117: `upd_matrix = left_vector.unsqueeze(1) @ right_vector.unsqueeze(0)`. left_vector is fp16 (post-P-8-ROME .to(u.dtype) downcast); right_vector is fp32 (compute_v's Adam optimization runs on fp32 delta parameters). The outer product matmul mismatches. P-9-ROME applies `.to(weight.dtype)` cast on BOTH operands at BOTH outer-product sites (execute_rome L117 + apply_rome_to_model L43). Same class as P-8-ROME (precision coercion), different file. Latent in upstream kmeng01/rome on any fp16 base model. ROME-only patch (MEMIT bypasses rome_main entirely via its own dispatch chain). v0.2 patches doc must add P-9-ROME as a sibling to P-8-ROME under SOURCE_LAYER patch class, ROME-only marker.
